evidence: The paper explicitly defines variable importance as "the dependence between input and output variables and computed by permuting the values of a given predictor and calculating error on a held out set." Though Figure 8 shows variable importance rankings ("the most important variable predicting demand is the average demand the week before"), this analysis is exploratory and secondary. The main conclusions concern predictive accuracy ("linear models often outperform machine learning methods") and ensemble robustness, not variable importance rankings. No impurity-based importance methods or statistical p-value procedures are mentioned.
